title: Helianthemum × guiraoi
intro: Helianthemum × guiraoi, also known as Rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the Helianthemum genus, native to the Iberian Peninsula, known for its vibrant pink flowers and fragrant, white-petaled foliage. The Rockrose plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum × guiraoi include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and skin-related ailments, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Helianthemum × guiraoi is a hybrid species within the genus Helianthemum, which is classified under the family Cistaceae. The genus Helianthemum is a group of flowering plants commonly known as rockroses. Helianthemum × guiraoi is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is a result of hybridization between two Helianthemum species. This hybrid species is often cultivated in gardens due to its attractive flowers and drought tolerance. The classification of Helianthemum × guiraoi reflects its unique origins and characteristics within the rockrose family.
